evidence: arxiv 2507.07101 'Small Batch Size Training for LMs' shows small batch = better per-FLOP + more robust to hyperparams. Never tried batch size changes.
notes: 2x more optimizer steps for same token budget. Smaller batch = noisier gradients but more updates. In undertrained regime (0.8 tok/param) more steps may help. grad_accum 16->8. No arch change = zero crash risk.

evidence: arxiv 2506.15025 'Optimal Embedding LR in LLMs' shows muP embed LR scaling may not match standard prescriptions. Never tuned embed LR.
notes: Currently embed_lr=0.4 (10x matrix_lr). Paper suggests ratio should scale as sqrt(width) not width. At width=768 this suggests lower embed LR. Risk: slower embedding learning.

evidence: Softcap 15->30 was catastrophic (f5a9cd9 +0.040). Tighter compression (12) may improve gradient signal quality.
notes: Softcap=15 was kept from modded-nanogpt. Never tried tighter. Tighter means logits compressed to [-12,12] which strengthens gradient on high-confidence tokens. Risk: may over-constrain.

idea: EMBEDDING_LR 0.4->0.8 (double embedding LR for faster embedding learning)
evidence: Undertrained regime: WD=0 success (6148421) showed less constraint helps. Faster embedding learning = better downstream features.
notes: Embeddings are input to everything — undertrained embeddings limit the whole model. Higher LR = faster feature learning. Risk: embedding instability (but muP should protect).
